title: Get Crawler Details
description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific crawler including input parameters and output fields

content.introduction:
This endpoint retrieves detailed information about a specific crawler using its hash ID. It helps you understand each crawler's availability, cost, parameters, and potential issues before using it.

{{RENDER:commonParameters}}

fields.description: Whether the crawler is publicly accessible
Whether the crawler is available for runs (false if paused)
Requires premium subscription or extra credits
Currently experiencing issues (runs paused until fixed)
Required account sync details (null if no authentication needed)
Array of configurable parameters (task-level and squid-level)
Array of output field names this crawler collects
Parameter level: "task" (per URL/task) or "squid" (overall run behavior)
For task-level: true indicates this parameter is used per task submission
For squid-level: true indicates this controls overall behavior or features
If true, this is an optional add-on feature with extra credit costs
Extra credits charged per row when this optional function is enabled
Parameter name to use when configuring the crawler
Data type of the parameter (string, int, boolean, etc.)
Default value if parameter is not provided

commonParameters.title: Common Squid-Level Parameters
commonParameters.content: Most crawlers share a set of common squid-level parameters returned in the `input` array. These control run behavior across all crawlers that support them:

rows.description: Maximum results per task. Leave null for unlimited.
Maximum unique results across all tasks in a run. Leave null for unlimited.
Only collect items published after this point. Accepts a relative duration (`24h`, `7d`, `2w`) or an absolute date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Replaces the deprecated `hours_back` and `max_date` parameters.
**Deprecated.** Use `fetch_since` instead. Previously filtered by hours.
**Deprecated.** Use `fetch_since` instead. Previously filtered by date.
Optional add-on features (e.g. email enrichment, image collection). Each key is a function name with a boolean value. Enabling a function incurs extra credits per row.

parameters.name: crawler_hash
parameters.description: The unique identifier (hash/id) of the crawler

proTips.content: Check the 'has_issues' flag before creating tasks. If true, the crawler is experiencing problems and runs are paused.
Parameters with 'function: true' are optional add-ons that cost extra credits per row (specified in 'credits_per_function').
Some crawlers require account synchronization (see 'account' field). You'll need to sync cookies before running these crawlers.
Use the 'result' array to know exactly what fields will be returned in your data. This helps you plan your data processing pipeline.
The `fetch_since` parameter replaces the deprecated `hours_back` and `max_date`. It accepts relative durations (24h, 7d, 2w) or absolute dates (YYYY-MM-DD).

seo.title: Get Crawler Details - lobstr.io API Documentation
seo.description: Learn how to retrieve detailed crawler information including input parameters, output fields, and pricing using the lobstr.io API.
